name: Scrapple
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Pork scraps with bones OR 1-hog's head, split
recipeInstructions:
Here's a Pennsylvania Dutch version of scrapple. You'll probably have better luck rounding up pork scraps than a pig's head, although you could probably pick one up at an Asian or Latino market.
1. Parboil pork scraps or head in water to cover for 10 minutes. Drain; discard water. Return to pot with fresh water to cover, salt, peppercorns, cloves and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then simmer until meat falls off the bones easily (about 3 hours).
2. Strain stock; reserve. Remove all meat from bones and chop; set aside.
3. Reheat stock; gradually add cornmeal, stirring constantly until thick (about 15 minutes). Add meat, onion, cayenne, nutmeg and sage; adjust seasonings with salt and pepper.
4. Pour into 2 greased loaf pans. Refrigerate until set. Slice and fry for breakfast. Will keep in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.
5. Serves 12.
